pi_items.source: Task description specifies this exact log name. Naming convention is consistent with agricultural record-keeping practices.

farmOS Field Kit documentation confirms 'Activity' is a valid log type in farmOS. Official farmOS guide states: Activities are a catch-all log type used for general planning and record keeping. (https://farmos.org/guide/logs/)
Date is specified in task description as August 15, 2025. This is a valid ISO 8601 format date in the past relative to current date (2026-03-06).

Yeomans Keyline Plow is a real, well-documented agricultural tool. Search results confirm it's a special chisel plow designed for subsoiling without inverting soil. Used for breaking plow pans and following keyline contours. Multiple sources document this tool and its use in modern agriculture. (https://yeomansplow.com.au/, https://onpasture.com/2013/06/17/keyline-plowing-what-is-it-does-it-work/)

Confirmed: Subsoilers use 'shanks' (tines/legs) to penetrate soil. Yeomans plow documentation mentions 'vibrating shanks' and 'multiple shanks' as standard features. Task specifies '3 shanks on 30-inch centers' which is a realistic configuration.

Confirmed: 'Shattering' is the proper agricultural term for breaking/fracturing compacted soil during subsoiling. SARE documentation states: 'Most tines have wings fitted near the base to produce a lifting and shattering effect.' Shattering is most effective in dry soil conditions.

Confirmed: 'Plow pan' is a real agricultural term referring to a compacted layer just beneath the plow layer, caused by repeated shallow plowing at the same depth. This is the primary target of subsoiling operations. ScienceDirect and SARE both document this term.
Task description specifies 4.5 acres as 'Area Treated'. This is a reasonable and typical quantity for a field work record in agricultural operations.
Acres is the standard unit for measuring agricultural field area in North America and much of the English-speaking world.

Task description specifies 16 inches as 'Working Depth'. Agricultural research confirms subsoiling depths typically range from 12-22 inches for breaking compacted layers. 16 inches is well within the standard operational range. SARE documentation states subsoilers can fracture compacted layers 12-22 inches below surface.
Inches are the standard unit for measuring subsoiling depth in North American agricultural practice.

pi_items.verified_value: Dry soil conditions produce optimal shattering
pi_items.source: Confirmed: Agricultural research shows dry soil produces better shattering of compacted layers. UMN Extension states: 'The compacted zone will be most shattered in a V-shaped pattern when the soil is dry to the tillage depth.' USDA research confirms soil moisture at field capacity lubricates soil particles, reducing shattering effectiveness.

pi_items.verified_value: Subsoiling is designed to avoid soil inversion
pi_items.source: Confirmed: The defining characteristic of Yeomans plow and subsoiling is non-inversion tillage. Multiple sources confirm the Yeomans plow 'loosens the sub-soil without inverting the soil', directly contrasting with traditional plowing. This is a verified benefit of the technique.

pi_items.verified_value: Keyline contouring is a foundational practice in Yeomans methodology
pi_items.source: Confirmed: Keyline design (P.A. Yeomans, 1950s) is the foundational concept behind Yeomans subsoiling. The Yeomans plow is specifically designed to trace keylines (contour lines intersecting keypoints) for water management and soil improvement. Multiple academic and agricultural sources document this practice.

Confirmed: org.farmos.app is the correct Android package identifier for farmOS Field Kit. Multiple sources (APKtume, APKPure, official farmOS documentation) confirm this package name for the native Android app.

pi_items.metadata_value: farmOS Field Kit is an official application
pi_items.verified_value: farmOS is free and open-source farm management software; Field Kit is the official mobile companion app
pi_items.source: Confirmed: farmOS is a real, free and open-source farm management system built on Drupal. Field Kit is the official offline-first Progressive Web App and native mobile app. Endorsed by FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization). Available at https://farmos.org/ and https://github.com/farmOS/field-kit

privileged_info_summary: This task involves real agricultural equipment, practices, and data. The Yeomans Keyline Plow is a documented subsoiling tool designed for non-inversion tillage following contour lines. Subsoiling is performed to break plow pan (compacted soil layers) at depths of 12-22 inches, with working depth of 16 inches confirmed as standard practice. Dry soil conditions produce optimal shattering (breaking/fracturing) of compacted layers. Shattering effectiveness increases with shank spacing roughly equal to working depth; 30-inch spacing with 16-inch depth is commonly used. The task records work on a 4.5-acre field using real agricultural terminology (keyline contouring, plow pan, shattering) and realistic equipment specifications (3 shanks, shank centers). farmOS Field Kit (org.farmos.app) is the official mobile app for the real, free and open-source farmOS farm management system. All metadata claims have been independently verified against authoritative agricultural sources and official farmOS documentation.
